function // applySubs:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word /id // #genPEG /href /a applySubs(wordmap:seq.word, p:seq.word) seq.word // //#replaceWords:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word /href replaceWords /a(p, wordmap)

function // applySubs:PEGtable:seq.word:seq.word /id // #genPEG /href /a applySubs(table:PEGtable, p:seq.word) seq.word
// let /keyword tmp = // //#run:PEGtable:seq.word:seq.word /href run /a(table, p)
// assert /keyword tmp // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1 // //#/href ∈ /a //"Match"/literal // report /keyword //"sub problem in genPEG::(p):(tmp)"/literal,
tmp // //#<<:seq.T:int:seq.T /href << /a 1

function // template:seq.seq.word /id // #genPEG /href /a template seq.seq.word
[// //"/wordsAttribute /else function =(seq.word, attributeType)boolean true /end"/literal
, //"function $(int)attributeType empty:seq.attributeType sub 1"/literal
, //"use standard"/literal
, //"use seq.tblEle"/literal
, //"use seq1.frame"/literal
, //"use stack.frame"/literal
, //"use seq1.attributeType"/literal
, //"use PEGrules"/literal
, //"/isWords /else type tblEle is action:state, match:seqElementType, Sstate:state, Fstate:state /error, recover:seq.word /end /end"/literal
, //"/error function recoveryEnding(rinfo:resultType /nogrammar, mytable:seq.tableEntry /end)seq.word /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
for acc =:(// //#/href dq /a.//""/literal), frame ∈ reverse.toseq.stk.rinfo do /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
if action.Sstate.frame ∈[T, T', NT]then acc+recover.mytable sub index.Sstate.frame else acc acc /else function place(r:resultType)int i.top.stk.r /end"/literal
, //"type frame is Sstate:state, Fstate:state, i:int, result:seq.attributeType, faili:int, failresult:seq.attributeType"/literal
, //"type resultType is stk:stack.frame /error, input:seq.seqElementType, place:int, faili:int /end /trace, trace:seq.word /end"/literal
, //"Function status(a:resultType)word if Sstate.top.stk.a ≠ Match then 'Failed else if place.a ={length of input}faili.top.stk.a then 'Match else 'MatchPrefix"/literal
, //"Function result(a:resultType)attributeType last.result.top.stk.a"/literal
, //"/trace function tracestart(trace:seq.word)seq.word:(// //#/href dq /a)/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp Key to column labels S--step no; D--Depth of Stack F--on fail reset I to F; I--Location in input; Lower case are values on top of stack /sp /tag <tr /tag > /nsp /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp
S /tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp D /tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp f /tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp success /tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp fail /tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp result /tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp Result /tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp F /tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp State /tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp I /tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp Remaining input /tag </td> /tag </tr> /sp /tag <tr /tag > /nsp /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp
$semicolon(trace):(// //#/href dq /a)/end"/literal
, //"/trace function trace(stepno:int, trace0:seq.word, stk:stack.frame, result:seq.attributeType, faili:int, state:state, i:int, input:seq.seqElementType /error, recovery:seq.word /end)seq.word let stkdata = %.n.toseq.stk+if isempty.stk then:(// //#/href dq /a)/tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp /tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp /tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp /tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp:(// //#/href dq /a)else:(// //#/href dq /a)/tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp $semicolon(faili.top.stk)/tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp $semicolon(Sstate.top.stk)/tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp $semicolon(Fstate.top.stk)/tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp $semicolon(last.result.top.stk):(// //#/href dq /a), trace0+:(// //#/href dq /a)$semicolon(stepno)/tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp $semicolon(stkdata)/tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp $semicolon(%(:(// //#/href dq /a)^:(// //#/href dq /a), result)>> 1)/tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp $semicolon(faili)/tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp $semicolon(state)/tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp $semicolon(i)/tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp $semicolon(subseq(input, i, min(i+20, n.input-1)))/tag </td> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp /error $semicolon(recovery)/tag </td> /end /tag </tr> /sp /tag <td /tag > /nsp
:(// //#/href dq /a)/end"/literal
, //"function parse(myinput0:seq.seqElementType, /nogrammar table:seq.tblEle, /else /end initAttr:attributeType /common, commonName:commonType /end /trace, startStep:int, endStep:int /end)resultType /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
let myinput = packed(myinput0+endMark)/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
let packedTable = packed./nogrammar table /else mytable /end /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
for /trace stepno = 1, trace0 =:(// //#/href dq /a):(// //#/href dq /a), /end /error rinfo = resultType(empty:stack.frame, myinput, 0, 1 /trace,:(// //#/href dq /a):(// //#/href dq /a)/end), /end stk = empty:stack.frame, state = startstate, i = 1, inputi = myinput sub 1, result =[initAttr], faili = 1, failresult =[initAttr]/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
while(toint.state > toint.Match /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/trace ∧ stepno ≤ endStep)do let trace = if stepno < startStep then trace0 else /error let tmp = if isempty.stk.rinfo then:(// //#/href dq /a.//""/literal)else:(// //#/href dq /a)$semicolon(place.rinfo)/tag </td> $semicolon(recoveryEnding(rinfo, table)):(// //#/href dq /a)/end trace(stepno, trace0, stk, result, faili, state, i, myinput /error, tmp /end)/else)do /end /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
let actionState = action.state /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
if actionState = Fail then{goto Fstate.top.stk, i = faili.top, pop.stk, discard result}/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
let top = top.stk /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
if toint.action.Fstate.top ≥ toint.S' then let newi = i.top next(/trace stepno+1, trace, /end /error rinfo, /end pop.stk, nextState.Fstate.top, newi, idxNB(myinput, newi), result.top, faili.top, failresult.top)else /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp

//{COMMAND PEGdebug /tag </strong> displays a trace of running a PEG. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
-/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp input /tag </strong> Expecting the first paragraph of input to be input and the second paragraph to be the grammar./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
-/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp steps /tag </strong> from to. Only display steps between from and to./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
-/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp notable /tag </strong>. Do not display the parse table or grammar.}/comment

, //"dq:(// //#/href dq /a), /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp /, //action /action, //br /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
"/literal

// // usegraph /id Module /keyword usegraph

use UTF8

use bits

use seq.byte

use seq.char

use file

use arc.modref

use graph.arc.modref

use seq.arc.modref

use set.arc.modref

use seq1.seq.modref

use seq1.modref

use set.modref

use seq1.set.modref

use stack.modref

use mytype

use seq.mytype

use seq1.mytype

use orderModules

use seq1.passsymbols

use set.passsymbols

use standard

use svg

use seq1.symbol

use set.symbol

use symbol1

use textio

use arc.word

use drawGraph.arc.word

use graph.arc.word

use seq.arc.word

use seq1.arc.word

use set.arc.word

use seq1.seq.word

use seq1.word

use set.word

Function // usegraph:seq.file:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word /id // #usegraph /href /a usegraph(
input:seq.file
, include:seq.word
, exclude:seq.word
, order:seq.word
) seq.word
//{COMMAND /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp PROFILE /tag </strong> usegraph /sp /tag <em /tag > /nsp graphs /tag </em> use relationship between modules in source code. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
options: /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp exclude /tag </strong> lists the modules to ignore in the use clauses. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp include /tag </strong> restricts the modules considered to those listed./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp order /tag </strong> finds ordering of modules so for any arc in graph, the tail occurs before the head. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp block"/tag > /nsp nograph Does not include graph in output. /tag </span> /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
Examples: /sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp block"/tag > /nsp usegraph /sp+built core.libsrc /sp /tag <a /sp href /nsp ="/nsp../documentation/Ex1usegraph.html"/tag > /nsp Result /tag </a> /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
> usegraph /sp+built core.libsrc exclude: seq standard /sp /tag <a /sp href /nsp ="/nsp../documentation/Ex2usegraph.html"/tag > /nsp Result /tag </a> /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
> usegraph /sp+built core.libsrc include: UTF8 words standard textio exclude: seq standard /sp /tag <a /sp href /nsp ="/nsp../documentation/Ex3usegraph.html"/tag > /nsp Result /tag </a> /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
> usegraph /sp+core UTF8.ls textio words standard encoding xxhash exclude = seq standard /tag </span> /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
The last two examples give the same result. The first excludes modules from consideration and the second only includes source files of modules that should be included. }/comment
// if /keyword // //#not:boolean:boolean /href not /a.isempty.order // then /keyword // // let /keyword allmodules = // // //#topasssymbols:seq.seq.word:seq.passsymbols /href topasssymbols /a.// //#breakparagraph:seq.file:seq.seq.word /href breakparagraph /a.input /block // for /keyword used = // //#empty::set.T:set.T /href empty:set.modref /a, e ∈ allmodules
// do /keyword // // if /keyword // //#isAbstract:modref:boolean /href isAbstract /a.modname.e // then /keyword used
// else /keyword used // //#/href ∪ /a uses.e // //#+:set.T:T:set.T /href+/a modname.e /block // let /keyword backarcs = // //#expand:seq.modref:set.passsymbols:boolean:set.arc.modref /href expand /a(toseq.used, // //#asset:seq.T:set.T /href asset /a.allmodules, true)
// let /keyword g0 = // //#newgraph:seq.T:graph.T /href newgraph /a.toseq.backarcs
// let /keyword before = // //#reverse:seq.T:seq.T /href reverse /a.orderNodes(nodes.g0, arcs.g0),
// let /keyword check = // //#check:seq.modref:seq.arc.modref:seq.word /href check /a(before, toseq.arcs.g0),
(// if /keyword isempty.check // then /keyword //"Good Ordering"/literal // else /keyword //"FailedOrdering:(check)"/literal)
// //#/href+/a {???? allow compound names in include such as encoding.seq.char}//"ordering::(before)"/literal
// //#/href+/a (// if /keyword //"nograph"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1 // //#/href ∈ /a order // then /keyword //""/literal
// else /keyword // // for /keyword wordarcs = empty:seq.arc.word, e ∈ toseq.arcs.g0
// do /keyword // // if /keyword head.e // //#=:modref:modref:boolean /href = /a tail.e // then /keyword wordarcs
// else /keyword // wordarcs
// //#/href+/a arc(// // //#/href merge /a.// //#%:modref:seq.word /href % /a.tail.e
, // //#/href merge /a.// //#%:modref:seq.word /href % /a.head.e /block), /block /block // //#drawgraph:seq.T:set.<<.T:set.<<.T:seq.word /href drawgraph /a(// wordarcs
, // //#asset:seq.T:set.T /href asset /a.include
, // //#asset:seq.T:set.T /href asset /a.exclude /block)/block)/block // else /keyword // // let /keyword arcs = // // //#usegraph:seq.seq.word:seq.arc.word /href usegraph /a.// //#breakparagraph:seq.file:seq.seq.word /href breakparagraph /a.input, /block // //#drawgraph:seq.T:set.<<.T:set.<<.T:seq.word /href drawgraph /a(// arcs
, // //#asset:seq.T:set.T /href asset /a.include
, // //#asset:seq.T:set.T /href asset /a.exclude /block)/block

function // %:set.modref:seq.word /id // #usegraph /href /a %(a:set.modref) seq.word // //#%:seq.T:seq.word /href % /a.toseq.a

function // check:seq.modref:seq.arc.modref:seq.word /id // #usegraph /href /a check(ord:seq.modref, arcs:seq.arc.modref) seq.word
//{verify ordering of modules}/comment
// for /keyword acc = //""/literal, a ∈ arcs
// do /keyword // // for /keyword state = //""/literal, grp = empty:seq.modref, m ∈ ord
// do /keyword // // if /keyword isempty.state // //#∧:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∧ /a name.m // //#/href ∈ /a //"("/literal // then /keyword next(//"group"/literal, grp)
// else /keyword if state = //"group"/literal // then /keyword // // if /keyword name.m // //#/href ∈ /a //")"/literal // then /keyword // // if /keyword head.a // //#/href ∈ /a grp // //#∧:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∧ /a tail.a // //#/href ∈ /a grp // then /keyword next(//"foundhead"/literal, empty:seq.modref)
// else /keyword if tail.a // //#/href ∈ /a grp // then /keyword next(//"foundtail"/literal, empty:seq.modref)
// else /keyword if head.a // //#/href ∈ /a grp // then /keyword next(//"problem"/literal, empty:seq.modref)
// else /keyword next(//""/literal, empty:seq.modref)/block // else /keyword next(state, grp // //#/href+/a m)/block // else /keyword if tail.a // //#=:modref:modref:boolean /href = /a m // //#∧:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∧ /a isempty.state // then /keyword next(//"foundtail"/literal, grp)
// else /keyword if head.a // //#=:modref:modref:boolean /href = /a m // then /keyword // if /keyword state = //"foundtail"/literal // then /keyword next(//"foundhead"/literal, grp) // else /keyword next(//"problem"/literal, grp)
// else /keyword next(state, grp), /block // if /keyword state = //"foundhead"/literal // then /keyword acc // else /keyword acc // //#/href+/a // //#%:arc.modref:seq.word /href % /a.a, /block acc

Function // usegraph:seq.seq.word:seq.arc.word /id // #usegraph /href /a usegraph(lib:seq.seq.word) seq.arc.word
// for /keyword // currentmod = //"?"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, result = empty:seq.arc.word
, p ∈ lib /block // do /keyword // // if /keyword isempty.p // then /keyword next(currentmod, result)
// else /keyword if p // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1 // //#/href ∈ /a //"module Module"/literal // then /keyword next(p // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 2, result)
// else /keyword if p // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1 // //#/href ∈ /a //"use"/literal // //#∧:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∧ /a n.p // //#>:int:int:boolean /href > /a 1 // then /keyword // // let /keyword m = p // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 2,
next(// currentmod
, // if /keyword currentmod // //#=:word:word:boolean /href = /a m // //#∨:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∨ /a currentmod // //#/href ∈ /a //"?"/literal // then /keyword result
// else /keyword result // //#/href+/a arc(currentmod, m)/block)/block // else /keyword next(currentmod, result), /block result

function // lookuptype:word:set.symbol:seq.mytype /id // #usegraph /href /a lookuptype(basetype:word, types:set.symbol) seq.mytype
// for /keyword found = empty:seq.mytype, e ∈ toseq.types
// while /keyword isempty.found
// do /keyword // // if /keyword // //#abstracttypename:mytype:word /href abstracttypename /a.(// //#nametype:symbol:seq.mytype /href nametype /a.e) // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
// //#=:word:word:boolean /href = /a basetype // then /keyword // //#nametype:symbol:seq.mytype /href nametype /a.e
// else /keyword found, /block found

function // resolvetype:seq.word:set.symbol:modref /id // #usegraph /href /a resolvetype(s:seq.word, types:set.symbol) modref
// let /keyword module = s // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 2,
// if /keyword n.s // //#<:int:int:boolean /href < /a 3
// //#∨:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∨ /a s // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 3 ∉ //"."/literal // then /keyword // //#moduleref:seq.word:modref /href moduleref /a(//"X"/literal // //#/href+/a module)
// else /keyword // // let /keyword basetype = s // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a n.s
// let /keyword bt = // // if /keyword basetype // //#/href ∈ /a //"T"/literal // then /keyword // //#typeT:mytype /href typeT /a
// else /keyword if basetype // //#/href ∈ /a //"int"/literal // then /keyword // //#typeint:mytype /href typeint /a
// else /keyword if basetype // //#/href ∈ /a //"real"/literal // then /keyword // //#typereal:mytype /href typereal /a
// else /keyword // // let /keyword found = // //#lookuptype:word:set.symbol:seq.mytype /href lookuptype /a(basetype, types),
// if /keyword isempty.found // then /keyword // //#newtype:modref:word:mytype /href newtype /a(// //#internalmod:modref /href internalmod /a, basetype)
// else /keyword found // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1, /block /block // if /keyword n.s // //#=:int:int:boolean /href = /a 4 // then /keyword // //#moduleref:seq.word:mytype:modref /href moduleref /a(//"X"/literal // //#/href+/a module, bt)
// else /keyword // // for /keyword // newtype = bt
, left = s // //#<<:seq.T:int:seq.T /href << /a 3 // //#>>:seq.T:int:seq.T /href >> /a 1 /block // while /keyword // //#not:boolean:boolean /href not /a.isempty.left
// do /keyword // // let /keyword e = left // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a n.left,
// if /keyword e // //#/href ∈ /a //"."/literal // then /keyword next(newtype, left // //#>>:seq.T:int:seq.T /href >> /a 1)
// else /keyword if e // //#/href ∈ /a //"seq"/literal // then /keyword next(// //#seqof:mytype:mytype /href seqof /a.newtype, left // //#>>:seq.T:int:seq.T /href >> /a 1)
// else /keyword // // let /keyword found = // //#lookuptype:word:set.symbol:seq.mytype /href lookuptype /a(e, types)
// let /keyword abstracttype = // // if /keyword isempty.found // then /keyword // //#typeref:seq.word:mytype /href typeref /a.[e, //"unknown"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1, //"X"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1]
// else /keyword found // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1, /block next(// // //#addabstract:mytype:mytype:mytype /href addabstract /a(abstracttype, newtype)
, left // //#>>:seq.T:int:seq.T /href >> /a 1 /block)/block /block // assert /keyword isempty.left // report /keyword //"XX:(s)"/literal,
// //#moduleref:seq.word:mytype:modref /href moduleref /a(//"X"/literal // //#/href+/a module, newtype)/block /block

function // %:arc.modref:seq.word /id // #usegraph /href /a %(a:arc.modref) seq.word //"{:(tail.a),:(head.a)}"/literal

function // topasssymbols:seq.seq.word:seq.passsymbols /id // #usegraph /href /a topasssymbols(mods:seq.seq.word) seq.passsymbols
// for /keyword // allmodules = empty:seq.passsymbols
, alltypes = // //#empty::set.T:set.T /href empty:set.symbol /a
, getuses ∈ [false, true]/block // do /keyword // // for /keyword // result1 = empty:seq.passsymbols
, thisModule = // //#internalmod:modref /href internalmod /a
, types = // //#empty::set.T:set.T /href empty:set.symbol /a
, uses = // //#empty::set.T:set.T /href empty:set.modref /a
, p ∈ mods // //#/href+/a //"Module ?"/literal /block // do /keyword // // if /keyword p // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1 // //#/href ∈ /a //"Module module"/literal // then /keyword // // let /keyword newmodule = // //#passsymbols:modref:set.modref:set.symbol:passsymbols /href passsymbols /a(thisModule, uses, types),
next(// result1 // //#/href+/a newmodule
, // //#resolvetype:seq.word:set.symbol:modref /href resolvetype /a(p, alltypes)
, // //#empty::set.T:set.T /href empty:set.symbol /a
, // //#empty::set.T:set.T /href empty:set.modref /a /block)/block // else /keyword if p // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1 // //#/href ∈ /a //"type"/literal // then /keyword // // let /keyword sym = // //#deepcopySym:mytype:symbol /href deepcopySym /a.// //#newtype:modref:word:mytype /href newtype /a(thisModule, p // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 2),
next(result1, thisModule, // //#asset:seq.T:set.T /href asset /a.[sym] // //#/href ∪ /a types, uses)/block // else /keyword if getuses // //#∧:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∧ /a p // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1 // //#/href ∈ /a //"use"/literal // then /keyword next(result1, thisModule, types, uses // //#+:set.T:T:set.T /href+/a // //#resolvetype:seq.word:set.symbol:modref /href resolvetype /a(p, alltypes))
// else /keyword next(result1, thisModule, types, uses)/block // for /keyword alltypes1 = // //#empty::set.T:set.T /href empty:set.symbol /a, m ∈ result1
// do /keyword alltypes1 // //#/href ∪ /a defines.m,
next(result1, alltypes1), /block allmodules

// // tauhelp /id Module /keyword tauhelp

Implements the tau help command, tauhelp, and buildhelp for extraction help information from the source.

use UTF8

use bits

use seq.byte

use seq.char

use file

use seq.file

use seq.filename

use seq.helpinfo

use pretty

use standard

use textio

use token

use seq1.seq.word

use sort.seq.word

use seq1.word

use set.word

use sort.word

type helpinfo is cmd:word, exe:word, txt:seq.word

function // %%:seq.file:seq.word /id // #tauhelp /href /a %%(s:seq.file) seq.word
// for /keyword // acc = //""/literal
, lastdir = //"built"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, lastext = //"?"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, e ∈ s /block // do /keyword // // let /keyword a = fn.e
// let /keyword t = // //#decodeword:word:seq.char /href decodeword /a.dirpath.a,
// let /keyword dir = // // if /keyword // //#subseq:seq.T:int:int:seq.T /href subseq /a(t, 1, 2)
// //#=:seq.T:seq.T:boolean /href = /a [// //#char1:seq.word:char /href char1 /a.//"."/literal, // //#char1:seq.word:char /href char1 /a.//"/"/literal] // then /keyword // //#encodeword:seq.char:word /href encodeword /a(t // //#<<:seq.T:int:seq.T /href << /a 2)
// else /keyword dirpath.a, /block next(// acc
// //#/href+/a (// if /keyword lastdir // //#=:word:word:boolean /href = /a dir // then /keyword //""/literal // else /keyword //"+"/literal // //#/href+/a dir)
// //#/href+/a name.a
// //#/href+/a (// if /keyword lastext // //#=:word:word:boolean /href = /a ext.a // then /keyword //""/literal // else /keyword //"."/literal // //#/href+/a ext.a)
, dir
, ext.a /block), /block acc

Function // buildhelp:seq.file:seq.file:boolean:boolean:seq.word /id // #tauhelp /href /a buildhelp(
input:seq.file
, target:seq.file
, shellscript:boolean
, doc:boolean
) seq.word
//{COMMAND /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp buildhelp /tag </strong> update helpdata in module. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp input /tag </strong> files from which to extract the help data./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp target /tag </strong> file to update help data in /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp shellscript /tag </strong> produces shell script to define commands. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp doc /tag </strong> format for command documentation source file. }/comment
// assert /keyword shellscript // //#∨:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∨ /a doc // //#∨:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∨ /a // //#not:boolean:boolean /href not /a.isempty.target // report /keyword //"Target must be specified."/literal
// for /keyword acc = empty:seq.seq.word, f ∈ input
// do /keyword // // for /keyword acc3 = acc, p ∈ // //#breakparagraph:seq.file:seq.seq.word /href breakparagraph /a.[f]
// do /keyword // // if /keyword p // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1 // //#/href ∈ /a //"Function"/literal // then /keyword // // let /keyword h = // //#getheader:seq.word:seq.word /href getheader /a.p
//{Header will include two additional words for formatting keywords Function}/comment
// if /keyword // //#subseq:seq.T:int:int:seq.T /href subseq /a(p, n.h // //#/href-/a 1, n.h)
= //"{COMMAND"/literal // then /keyword // acc3
// //#/href+/a ([p // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 2]
// //#/href+/a name.fn.f
// //#/href+/a // //#escapeformat:word /href escapeformat /a
// //#/href+/a // //#subseq:seq.T:int:int:seq.T /href subseq /a(p, 3, // //#findindex:seq.T:T:int /href findindex /a(p, //"}"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1))
// //#/href+/a // //#escapeformat:word /href escapeformat /a)/block // else /keyword acc3 /block // else /keyword acc3, /block acc3 /block // for /keyword info = empty:seq.helpinfo, e ∈ // //#sort>alpha:seq.T:seq.T /href sort>alpha /a.acc
// do /keyword info // //#/href+/a helpinfo(e // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1, e // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 2, e // //#<<:seq.T:int:seq.T /href << /a 2),
// if /keyword doc // then /keyword // // let /keyword esc = [// //#escapeformat:word /href escapeformat /a]
// for /keyword txt = //""/literal, e ∈ info
// do /keyword // // let /keyword j = // //#findindex:seq.T:T:int /href findindex /a(txt.e, //"COMMAND"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1)
// let /keyword jend = // //#findindex:seq.T:T:int /href findindex /a(txt.e, //"}"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1),
txt
// //#/href+/a //"/sp /tag <h3 /tag > /nsp Command:(cmd.e)/tag </h3> /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp:(// //#subseq:seq.T:int:int:seq.T /href subseq /a(txt.e, j // //#+:int:int:int /href+/a 1, jend // //#/href-/a 1))"/literal, /block txt /block // else /keyword if shellscript // then /keyword // //#script:seq.helpinfo:seq.word /href script /a.info
// else /keyword // // let /keyword discard = // //#tknencoding:seq.word /href tknencoding /a
// for /keyword // txt = //"function helpdata seq.helpinfo /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
{This function was created using /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
buildhelp:(// //#%%:seq.file:seq.word /href %% /a.input)target::(// //#%%:seq.file:seq.word /href %% /a.target)}/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
["/literal
, e ∈ info /block // do /keyword // txt
// //#/href+/a //"helpinfo(:(// //#/href dq /a.[cmd.e])sub 1,:(// //#/href dq /a.[exe.e])sub 1,:(// //#/href dq /a.// //#escapedq:seq.word:seq.word /href escapedq /a.txt.e)), /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
"/literal /block // let /keyword newdata = txt // //#>>:seq.T:int:seq.T /href >> /a 2 // //#/href+/a //"]"/literal,
// for /keyword // txt2 = //""/literal
, p ∈ // //#breakparagraph:seq.file:seq.seq.word /href breakparagraph /a.[target // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1]/block // do /keyword // // if /keyword txt2 = //""/literal // //#∧:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∧ /a // //#subseq:seq.T:int:int:seq.T /href subseq /a(p, 1, 2) = //"# File"/literal // then /keyword txt2
// else /keyword // txt2
// //#/href+/a (// if /keyword // //#subseq:seq.T:int:int:seq.T /href subseq /a(p, 1, 2) = //"function helpdata"/literal // then /keyword newdata // //#/href+/a //"/sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp"/literal
// else /keyword if p // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1 // //#/href ∈ /a //"function Function"/literal // then /keyword // // //#removeMarkup:seq.word:seq.word /href removeMarkup /a.// //#pretty:seq.word:seq.word /href pretty /a.p
// //#/href+/a //"/sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp"/literal /block // else /keyword p // //#/href+/a //""/literal), /block /block txt2 /block

function // escapedq:seq.word:seq.word /id // #tauhelp /href /a escapedq(s:seq.word) seq.word
// for /keyword acc = //""/literal, e ∈ s
// do /keyword // if /keyword e // //#/href ∈ /a // //#/href dq /a // then /keyword acc // //#/href+/a //"^"/literal // //#/href+/a //"(dq)"/literal // else /keyword acc // //#/href+/a e,
acc

Function // tauhelp:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word /id // #tauhelp /href /a tauhelp(cmd:seq.word, %:seq.word) seq.word
//{COMMAND /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp tauhelp /tag </strong> Help documentation for commands. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
This information is extracted from the comment in the source code for the command. If no option is supplied a description of the commands are printed./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp cmd /tag </strong> restrict what is displayed. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
"cmd: help1"will display documentation for tauhelp only and"cmd: help1 out"will display only the documentation of the out option of the tauhelp command. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
Options: /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp /tag </strong> % format of output. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp shellscript /tag </strong> creates a shell script for commands./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp full /tag </strong> Full documentations of commands /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp brief /tag </strong> Show extract of /sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp block"/tag > /nsp documentations /tag </span>}/comment
// let /keyword cmd0 = // //#subseq:seq.T:int:int:seq.T /href subseq /a(cmd, 1, 1)
// let /keyword option = // //#subseq:seq.T:int:int:seq.T /href subseq /a(cmd, 2, 2),
// if /keyword % = //"shellscript"/literal // then /keyword // //#script:seq.helpinfo:seq.word /href script /a.// //#helpdata:seq.helpinfo /href helpdata /a
// else /keyword // // for /keyword acc = //""/literal, c = 1, p ∈ // //#helpdata:seq.helpinfo /href helpdata /a
// do /keyword // next(// // if /keyword isempty.cmd0 // //#∨:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∨ /a cmd.p // //#/href ∈ /a cmd0 // then /keyword acc // //#/href+/a // //#hh:helpinfo:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word /href hh /a(p, %, option) // //#/href+/a //"/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp"/literal // else /keyword acc
, c // //#+:int:int:int /href+/a 1 /block), /block // if /keyword isempty.% // then /keyword // //"For more information on command use tauhelp cmd: <command> %: full /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
:(acc)"/literal /block // else /keyword acc /block

function // script:seq.helpinfo:seq.word /id // #tauhelp /href /a script(s:seq.helpinfo) seq.word
// for /keyword acc = //""/literal, p ∈ s
// do /keyword // // let /keyword i = // //#findindex:seq.T:T:int /href findindex /a(txt.p, //":"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1)
// let /keyword defaultDir = // // if /keyword // //#subseq:seq.T:int:int:seq.T /href subseq /a(txt.p, i // //#/href-/a 1, i // //#/href-/a 1)
= //"input"/literal // then /keyword //"+built"/literal
// else /keyword //""/literal, /block acc
// //#/href+/a //"/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp function:(cmd.p)/sp{/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
tau:(exe.p):(cmd.p):(defaultDir)$@ /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
}"/literal, /block //"# functions are used instead of alias because alias adds extra arguments /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
:(acc)"/literal

function // paradesc:helpinfo:seq.word /id // #tauhelp /href /a paradesc(d:helpinfo) seq.word
// for /keyword acc2 = //""/literal, last = //"X"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1, w ∈ txt.d
// while /keyword w ∉ //"{}"/literal
// do /keyword next(// if /keyword w // //#/href ∈ /a //":"/literal // then /keyword acc2 // //#/href+/a last // //#/href+/a //","/literal // else /keyword acc2, w),
// if /keyword isempty.acc2 // then /keyword //""/literal // else /keyword //"(:(acc2))"/literal

function // hh:helpinfo:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word /id // #tauhelp /href /a hh(d:helpinfo, level:seq.word, option:seq.word) seq.word
// if /keyword level = //"full"/literal // then /keyword // // let /keyword j = // //#findindex:seq.T:T:int /href findindex /a(txt.d, //"COMMAND"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1),
//"/sp /tag <h3 /tag > /nsp Command:(cmd.d)/tag </h3> /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp:(// //#subseq:seq.T:int:int:seq.T /href subseq /a(txt.d, j // //#+:int:int:int /href+/a 1, n.txt.d // //#/href-/a 1))"/literal /block // else /keyword // // let /keyword a = //"kl"/literal,
//":(cmd.d) /tag </strong>:(// //#/href break /a(a, //"/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp"/literal, true) // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 2 // //#<<:seq.T:int:seq.T /href << /a 3)"/literal /block

function // helpdata:seq.helpinfo /id // #tauhelp /href /a helpdata seq.helpinfo
//{This function was created using buildhelp tools.libsrc taubc webassembly transform1info.html target:+tools tauhelp.ls}/comment
[// helpinfo(// //"PEGdebug"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"tools"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"(input:seq.file, steps:seq.word, notable:boolean)seq.word{COMMAND /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp PEGdebug /tag </strong> displays a trace of running a PEG. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
-/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp input /tag </strong> Expecting the first paragraph of input to be input and the second paragraph to be the grammar./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
-/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp steps /tag </strong> from to. Only display steps between from and to./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
-/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp notable /tag </strong>. Do not display the parse table or grammar.}"/literal /block)
, helpinfo(// //"buildhelp"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"tools"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"(input:seq.file, target:seq.file, shellscript:boolean, doc:boolean)seq.word{COMMAND /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp buildhelp /tag </strong> update helpdata in module. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp input /tag </strong> files from which to extract the help data./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp target /tag </strong> file to update help data in /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp shellscript /tag </strong> produces shell script to define commands. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp doc /tag </strong> format for command documentation source file. }"/literal /block)
, helpinfo(// //"entrypoint"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"taubc"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"(input:seq.file, entryUses:seq.word, core:boolean)seq.byte{COMMAND /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp entrypoint /tag </strong> For seeing the Module the compiler generates to define the entry point. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
Options:/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp entryUses /tag </strong> addition use clause added to module when building entry point. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp core /tag </strong> Only show core of Module. }"/literal /block)
, helpinfo(// //"front"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"tools"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //":T(input:seq.file, output:seq.word, pass:seq.word, n:seq.word, ~n:seq.word, mods:seq.word, ~mods:seq.word, within:boolean, %:seq.word)seq.file{COMMAND The /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp front /tag </strong> is a multiple-purpose command. It outputs data from various stages of the compiler. /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
One use is to determine which functions are used between modules. The usegraph of the core functions indicates there are dependencies between the modules texio, file and bits. To see the dependences, use /sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp block"/tag > /nsp block front /sp+built core.libsrc mods: textio file bits format /tag </span> A graph will be displayed with the dependencies between the modules. The nodes in the graph are the procedure names. Since a name does not uniquely identify a function, hovering over the beginning of the name will pop up a more complete description beginning with the name of the function. /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
The dependence on the module bits will not be displayed. If an earlier pass of the compiler is specified like this /sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp block"/tag > /nsp front  +built taubc.libsrc mods = textio file bits format pass = text /tag </span> then it will be displayed. /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
The dependence within the module textio can be seen with /sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp block"/tag > /nsp front  +built taubc.libsrc mods = textio pass = text within = /tag </span> /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
To see all the functions that call functions /sp /tag <em /tag > /nsp named /tag </em> breakparagraph in the library use /sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp block"/tag > /nsp front  +built taubc.libsrc n = breakparagraph pass = text out = calledby /tag </span> /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
This will list the function definitions in a package /sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp block"/tag > /nsp front  +built taubc.libsrc mods = textio out = symdef /tag </span> The format is the function followed by a post order transversal of the call tree. /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
The front command takes several parameters that control which functions are considered./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp ○ /tag </strong> n a list of names of functions to include /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp ○ /tag </strong> ~n a list of names to exclude /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp ○ /tag </strong> mods a list of modules to include /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp ○ /tag </strong> ~mods a list of modules to exculde /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp ○ /tag </strong> pass The option pass determines how much processing is done before looking at the symbols. /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp • /tag </strong> library Only report on functions imported from libraries. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp • /tag </strong> text Parse the input in such a way that the source code can be reconstructed./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp • /tag </strong> pass1 Output from first stage of processing. All bindings of text to symbols have been done./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp • /tag </strong> pass1a Like pass1 with Compiler options on Export statements added. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp • /tag </strong> pass2 After some optimization /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp • /tag </strong> all Just before code generation /sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp block"/tag > /nsp. /tag </span>
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp ○ /tag </strong> % The option % determines the format of the output. /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp • /tag </strong> sym Just the symbol names /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp • /tag </strong> symdef The symbol definitions. The format is the symbol followed by a post order transversal of the call tree./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp • /tag </strong> symdefgraph For each symbol definition, the definition is presented as a call tree graph./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp • /tag </strong> calledby The option n is ignored in building a call graph. Then only the symbols that • call symbols in n directly or indirectly are included in the graph /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp • /tag </strong> calls The option n is ignored in building a call graph. Then only the symbols that • are called(directly or indirectly)from symbols in n are included in the graph./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp • /tag </strong> txt Instead of producing a SVG graph print the args of the graph./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp • /tag </strong> baseTypeCheck /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp • /tag </strong> /sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp block"/tag > /nsp resultCheck /tag </span>}"/literal /block)
, helpinfo(// //"makeScript"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"taubc"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"(input:seq.file, builddir:seq.word, hashes:seq.file, output:seq.word)seq.byte{COMMAND /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp makeScript /tag </strong> creates shell script for building code./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
The commands' primary is is in the shell script taubuild./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp input /tag </strong> build files /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp builddir /tag </strong> build directory, usually ^(dq)+built ^(dq)./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp hashes /tag </strong> two files with one containing lines from the unix command shasum. The two files are compared looking for identical lines which is used to determine which files have not be changed since the last build.}"/literal /block)
, helpinfo(// //"makebitcode"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"taubc"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"(input:seq.file, exports:seq.word, info:boolean, profile:boolean, showllvm:seq.word, entryUses:seq.word, output:seq.word)seq.file{COMMAND /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp makebitcode /tag </strong> compiler /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
Options:/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp entryUses /tag </strong> addition use clause added to module when building entry point. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp profile /tag </strong> generates information for profiling /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp showllvm /tag </strong> show text form of llvm code generated for the names of procedure given./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp info /tag </strong> show text form of code info /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/exports list of packes to show. }"/literal /block)
, helpinfo(// //"prettyScript"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"tools"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"(input:seq.file, builddir:seq.word, hashes:seq.file, roots:seq.word, %:seq.word)seq.word{COMMAND /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp prettyScript /tag </strong> pretty prints /sp.bld script./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp % /tag </strong> format of output. /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
pretty /tag </strong> ? /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp full /tag </strong> Graph of dependences between files./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp outdated /tag </strong> like full but only include outdated files in graph /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp script /tag </strong> shell script for updating without the file hashes /sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp block"/tag > /nsp. /tag </span> /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
options used for formats other than pretty./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp roots /tag </strong> only include the filenames list and their descendants in graph. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp hashes /tag </strong> ? /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp builddir /tag </strong> build target directory. Defaults to ^(dq)+built ^(dq)}"/literal /block)
, helpinfo(// //"tauhelp"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"tools"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"(cmd:seq.word, %:seq.word)seq.word{COMMAND /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp tauhelp /tag </strong> Help documentation for commands. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
This information is extracted from the comment in the source code for the command. If no option is supplied a description of the commands are printed./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp cmd /tag </strong> restrict what is displayed. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
^(dq)cmd: help1 ^(dq)will display documentation for tauhelp only and ^(dq)cmd: help1 out ^(dq)will display only the documentation of the out option of the tauhelp command. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
Options: /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp /tag </strong> % format of output. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp shellscript /tag </strong> creates a shell script for commands./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp full /tag </strong> Full documentations of commands /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp brief /tag </strong> Show extract of /sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp block"/tag > /nsp documentations /tag </span>}"/literal /block)
, helpinfo(// //"transform"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"tools"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //":T(input:seq.file, link:seq.file, output:seq.word, target:seq.word, modrename:seq.word, bind:boolean, reorguse:boolean, html:seq.word, cleanexports:boolean, moveexports:boolean, patternmods:seq.word)seq.file{COMMAND The /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp transform /tag </strong> cmd takes a list of input source files. For each module in the input, a pretty printed file is created in the directory <Tau>/tmp. Additional parameters allow for different variants. /sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp block"/tag > /nsp transform+tests profileExample.ls helloworld.ls /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
transform+tests profileExample.ls reorguse:/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
transform  +built profileExample.libsrc taubc.libinfo bind:/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
transform  +built profileExample.libsrc taubc.libinfo bind: reorguse: /tag </span> /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
This first variant does not require the source to be semantically correct, but the syntax must be correct. It does not change the order of the paragraphs. /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
The second is like the first except that it moves the use paragraphs to the beginning of the module, removes duplicates, and sorts them./sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
The third is like the first but requires correct semantics. This allows some additional transformations such as /sp /tag <em /tag > /nsp not(a = b)/tag </em> to become /sp /tag <em /tag > /nsp a ≠ b /tag </em>. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
○ /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp target /tag </strong> overides default target directory tmp /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
○ /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp bind /tag </strong> Does further processing of input to bind id's to symbols./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
○ /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp reorguse /tag </strong> Reorganizes use clauses. If /sp /tag <em /tag > /nsp bind /tag </em> is also specified, unnecessary use clauses are removed./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
○ /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp link /tag </strong> A list of file names ending with /sp.html that will not be compiled but will be scanned for functions that are called by the files. Hyperlinks will be create from the location of the call to the definition in the link files. Files without a.html extenstion are ignored. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
○ /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp html /tag </strong> An html file is produced with an index of modules. This option is useful for examining source code and contains for producing documentation with imbedded Tau code.This options contains one or more of the following. /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp • Module /tag </strong> Include modules in the table of contents. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
• /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp headersOnly /tag </strong> Only include the headers of exported functions in result./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
• /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp h1 /tag </strong> include html h1 headers in table of contents /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
• /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp h2 /tag </strong> include html h2 headers in table of contents /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
• /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp h3 /tag </strong> include html h3 headers in table of contents /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
• /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp h4 /tag </strong> include html h4 headers in table of contents /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
• /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp h5 /tag </strong> include html h5 headers in table of contents /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
• /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp h6 /tag </strong> include html h6 headers in table of /sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp block"/tag > /nsp contents /tag </span>
○ /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp modrename /tag </strong> List of modules to rename in form: oldname1 newname1 oldname2 newname2... /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
○ /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp patternmods /tag </strong> List of modules that contains patterns. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
See /sp /tag <a /sp href /nsp ="/nsp # /nsp patterns"/tag > /nsp Example /tag </a> /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
○ /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp cleanexports /tag </strong> Remove exports for exports in module or if Export is from another module add comment to indicate where it comes from./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
○ /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp moveexports /tag </strong> Move all exports to just after use clauses./sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
The /sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp keyword"/tag > /nsp transform /tag </span> command treats the function genEnum as magic and will generate code in a module for enumeration types instead of creating the code by hand. /sp /tag <a /sp href /nsp ="/nsp # /nsp enum"/tag > /nsp genEnum Example /tag </a>}"/literal /block)
, helpinfo(// //"unusedsymbols"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"tools"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //":T(input:seq.file, all:boolean, generated:boolean, excessExports:boolean, ignore:seq.word)seq.word{COMMAND /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp unusedsymbols /tag </strong> cmd analyzes code looking for unused functions. /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
It forms the function call graph for the program. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
It then looks for any sources in the call graph that are not the entry point of the program and lists them. Any functions that are generated from type definitions are also removed. /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
Here is an example tau /sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp block"/tag > /nsp tools unusedsymbols /sp+built tools.libsrc taubc.libinfo /tag </span> /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
The behavior can be modified with flags. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
-/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp all /tag </strong> list all unused functions--not just the roots. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
-/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp generated /tag </strong> The symbols generated from type definitions are included. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
-/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp excessExports /tag </strong> list symbols exported from a module but only used internally to that module./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
-/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp ignore /tag </strong> Symbols with these names are not listed in the output. Default value is genEnum genPEG}"/literal /block)
, helpinfo(// //"usegraph"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"tools"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"(input:seq.file, include:seq.word, exclude:seq.word, order:seq.word)seq.word{COMMAND /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp PROFILE /tag </strong> usegraph /sp /tag <em /tag > /nsp graphs /tag </em> use relationship between modules in source code. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
options: /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp exclude /tag </strong> lists the modules to ignore in the use clauses. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp include /tag </strong> restricts the modules considered to those listed./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp order /tag </strong> finds ordering of modules so for any arc in graph, the tail occurs before the head. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp block"/tag > /nsp nograph Does not include graph in output. /tag </span> /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
Examples: /sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp block"/tag > /nsp usegraph /sp+built core.libsrc /sp /tag <a /sp href /nsp ="/nsp../documentation/Ex1usegraph.html"/tag > /nsp Result /tag </a> /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
> usegraph /sp+built core.libsrc exclude: seq standard /sp /tag <a /sp href /nsp ="/nsp../documentation/Ex2usegraph.html"/tag > /nsp Result /tag </a> /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
> usegraph /sp+built core.libsrc include: UTF8 words standard textio exclude: seq standard /sp /tag <a /sp href /nsp ="/nsp../documentation/Ex3usegraph.html"/tag > /nsp Result /tag </a> /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
> usegraph /sp+core UTF8.ls textio words standard encoding xxhash exclude = seq standard /tag </span> /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
The last two examples give the same result. The first excludes modules from consideration and the second only includes source files of modules that should be included. }"/literal /block)
, helpinfo(// //"wasm"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"webassembly"/literal // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, //"(input:seq.file, Library:seq.word, exports:seq.word, output:seq.word, info:boolean, profile:boolean)seq.file{COMMAND compile to webAssembly /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
Library /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
exports /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
output /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
profile /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
}"/literal /block)/block]

// // PEGgenNoTable /id Module /keyword PEGgenNoTable

use PEGrules

use standard

use arc.state

use graph.arc.state

use seq.arc.state

use set.arc.state

use seq1.state

use set.state

use seq.tableEntry

type convertInfo is
table:seq.tableEntry
, start:state
, newStates:set.state
, actions:seq.seq.word
, wordmap:seq.word

Function // cvt:convertInfo:state:int:int /id // #PEGgenNoTable /href /a cvt(a:convertInfo, s:state, place:int) int
// let /keyword kk = // // if /keyword s // //#=:state:state:boolean /href = /a start.a // then /keyword 2
// else /keyword // //#findindex:set.T:T:int /href findindex /a(newStates.a, s) // //#+:int:int:int /href+/a 2 /block // assert /keyword kk // //#>:int:int:boolean /href > /a 1 // report /keyword //"FFF:(place):(s)/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp newStates::(toseq.newStates.a):(// //#%table:seq.tableEntry:seq.word /href %table /a.table.a)"/literal,
kk

function // normalize:state:seq.tableEntry:state /id // #PEGgenNoTable /href /a normalize(s:state, table:seq.tableEntry) state
// let /keyword tmp = // // //#action:state:state /href action /a.action.table // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a // //#index:state:int /href index /a.s, /block // if /keyword tmp // //#=:state:state:boolean /href = /a // //#/href NT /a // then /keyword // //#NT:int:state /href NT /a.// //#index:state:int /href index /a.s
// else /keyword if tmp // //#=:state:state:boolean /href = /a // //#T':state /href T' /a // then /keyword // //#T':int:state /href T' /a.// //#index:state:int /href index /a.s
// else /keyword if tmp // //#=:state:state:boolean /href = /a // //#!T:state /href !T /a // then /keyword // //#!T:int:state /href !T /a.// //#index:state:int /href index /a.s
// else /keyword // // assert /keyword tmp // //#=:state:state:boolean /href = /a // //#/href T /a // report /keyword //"DFDS:(tmp)"/literal,
// //#/href T /a.// //#index:state:int /href index /a.s /block

function // fixit:seq.word:int:seq.word /id // #PEGgenNoTable /href /a fixit(r:seq.word, noNT:int) seq.word
// if /keyword n.r // //#<:int:int:boolean /href < /a 3 // then /keyword r
// else /keyword // // let /keyword none = // //#/href merge /a.//".."/literal
// for /keyword acc = //""/literal, l2 = none, l1 = none, e ∈ r // //#/href+/a //".."/literal
// do /keyword // // if /keyword l1 // //#/href ∈ /a //"."/literal // //#∧:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∧ /a l2 // //#/href ∈ /a //"$"/literal // then /keyword // // let /keyword tmp = noNT // //#/href-/a // //#toint:word:int /href toint /a.e // //#/href-/a 1
// let /keyword new = // if /keyword tmp // //#=:int:int:boolean /href = /a 0 // then /keyword //"(R sub n.R)"/literal // else /keyword //"(R sub(n.R-:(tmp)))"/literal,
next(acc // //#/href+/a new, none, none)/block // else /keyword // next(// // if /keyword l2 // //#=:word:word:boolean /href = /a none // then /keyword acc // else /keyword acc // //#/href+/a l2
, l1
, e /block), /block /block acc /block

Function // notable:seq.pegrule:seq.tableEntry:seq.word:seq.seq.word /id // #PEGgenNoTable /href /a notable(grammar:seq.pegrule, table:seq.tableEntry, wordmap:seq.word) seq.seq.word
// for /keyword // begins = // //#empty::set.T:set.T /href empty:set.state /a
, actions = [//"unused"/literal]
, r ∈ grammar /block // do /keyword // // for /keyword actions1 = actions, count = 1, p ∈ parts.r
// do /keyword // next(// actions1
// //#/href+/a //"{:((// if /keyword kind.r // //#/href ∈ /a //"*+"/literal // then /keyword [kind.r] // else /keyword //""/literal)):(leftside.r):(count)}:(// //#fixit:seq.word:int:seq.word /href fixit /a(replacement.p, // //#NTcount:pegpart:seq.pegrule:int /href NTcount /a(p, grammar) // //#+:int:int:int /href+/a 1))"/literal
, count // //#+:int:int:int /href+/a 1 /block), /block next(// begins // //#+:set.T:T:set.T /href+/a // //#normalize:state:seq.tableEntry:state /href normalize /a(begin.r, table)
, actions1 /block)/block // for /keyword toprocess = begins, g = // //#newgraph:seq.T:graph.T /href newgraph /a.empty:seq.arc.state
// while /keyword // //#not:boolean:boolean /href not /a.// //#isempty:set.T:boolean /href isempty /a.toprocess
// do /keyword // // for /keyword arcs = empty:seq.arc.state, i ∈ toseq.toprocess
// do /keyword // // let /keyword tmp = // //#action:state:state /href action /a.i,
// if /keyword tmp
// //#/href ∈ /a [// // //#Success*:state /href Success* /a
, // //#Fail:state /href Fail /a
, // //#Reduce:state /href Reduce /a
, // //#All:state /href All /a /block] // then /keyword arcs
// else /keyword if tmp // //#/href ∈ /a [// //#Reduce*:state /href Reduce* /a, // //#/href Discard* /a] // then /keyword arcs // //#/href+/a arc(i, // //#nextState:state:state /href nextState /a.i)
// else /keyword // // assert /keyword tmp
// //#/href ∈ /a [// // //#/href T /a
, // //#/href NT /a
, // //#T':state /href T' /a
, // //#/href S' /a
, // //#!T:state /href !T /a
, // //#MatchAny:state /href MatchAny /a /block] // report /keyword //"HHH:(i)"/literal
// let /keyword te = table // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a // //#index:state:int /href index /a.i,
arcs
// //#/href+/a arc(i, Sstate.te)
// //#/href+/a arc(i, Fstate.te)
// //#/href+/a (// if /keyword // //#action:state:state /href action /a.action.te // //#/href ∈ /a [// //#/href NT /a, // //#/href NT* /a] // then /keyword[arc(i, // //#nextState:state:state /href nextState /a.action.te)]
// else /keyword empty:seq.arc.state)
// //#/href+/a (// if /keyword // //#action:state:state /href action /a.Sstate.te
// //#/href ∈ /a [// //#/href Discard* /a, // //#Reduce*:state /href Reduce* /a] // then /keyword[arc(i, // //#nextState:state:state /href nextState /a.Sstate.te)]
// else /keyword empty:seq.arc.state)/block /block // let /keyword newg = g // //#/href+/a arcs,
next(nodes.newg // //#\:set.T:set.T:set.T /href \ /a (nodes.g // //#/href ∪ /a toprocess), newg)/block // for /keyword stacked = // //#empty::set.T:set.T /href empty:set.state /a, e ∈ table
// do /keyword // // if /keyword // //#action:state:state /href action /a.action.e // //#/href ∈ /a [// //#/href NT /a, // //#/href NT* /a] // then /keyword stacked // //#+:set.T:T:set.T /href+/a Fstate.e // //#+:set.T:T:set.T /href+/a Sstate.e
// else /keyword stacked /block // let /keyword start = // //#T':int:state /href T' /a.2
// for /keyword newStates = // //#empty::set.T:set.T /href empty:set.state /a, e ∈ toseq.nodes.g
// do /keyword // // let /keyword in = // //#indegree:graph.T:<<.T:int /href indegree /a(g, e),
// if /keyword in // //#=:int:int:boolean /href = /a 1 // //#∧:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∧ /a e ∉ stacked // //#∧:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∧ /a e ∉ begins
// //#∨:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∨ /a in // //#=:int:int:boolean /href = /a 0
// //#∨:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∨ /a e // //#=:state:state:boolean /href = /a start // then /keyword newStates
// else /keyword newStates // //#+:set.T:T:set.T /href+/a e /block // let /keyword info = convertInfo(table, start, newStates, actions, wordmap)
// for /keyword code = //""/literal, j ∈ [start] // //#/href+/a toseq.newStates
// do /keyword // code
// //#/href+/a (// if /keyword j ≠ (toseq.newStates) // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a // //#n:set.T:int /href n /a.newStates // then /keyword //"if state ={:(j)}:(// //#cvt:convertInfo:state:int:int /href cvt /a(info, j, 2))then"/literal
// else /keyword //"assert state ={:(j)}:(// //#cvt:convertInfo:state:int:int /href cvt /a(info, j, 3))report:(// //#/href dq /a.//"???"/literal)"/literal)
// //#/href+/a // //#build:int:convertInfo:state:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word /href build /a(0, info, j, //"stk"/literal, //"i"/literal, //"inputi"/literal, //"result"/literal, //"faili"/literal, //"failini"/literal, //"failresult"/literal)
// //#/href+/a //"else"/literal, /block[// //"/wordsAttribute /else function =(seq.word, attributeType)boolean true /end"/literal
, //"function $(int)attributeType empty:seq.attributeType sub 0"/literal
, //"type frame is Sstate:int, Fstate:int, i:int, result:seq.attributeType, faili:int, failini:seqElementType, failresult:seq.attributeType"/literal
, //"type resultType is stk:stack.frame /trace, trace:seq.word /end"/literal
, //"Function result(a:resultType)attributeType last.result.top.stk.a"/literal
, //"function parse(myinput0:seq.seqElementType, initAttr:attributeType /common, common:commonType /end)resultType /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
let myinput = packed(myinput0+endMark)/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
let initresult =[initAttr]let initstk = push(empty:stack.frame, frame(1, 0, 1, initresult, 1, myinput sub 1, initresult))/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
for /trace stepno = 1, trace0 =:(// //#/href dq /a.//""/literal), /end stk = initstk, state = 2, i = 1, inputi = myinput sub 1, result = initresult, faili = 1, failini = myinput sub 1, failresult = initresult /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
while state > toint.Match do /trace let trace = trace0 /end:(code // //#>>:seq.T:int:seq.T /href >> /a 1)/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp resultType(push(stk, frame(state, state, i, result, n.myinput, inputi, result))/trace, trace0 /end)"/literal /block]

function // replaceWords2:word:seq.word:seq.word /id // #PEGgenNoTable /href /a replaceWords2(a:word, b:seq.word) seq.word
// if /keyword a // //#/href ∈ /a //"break"/literal // then /keyword //"break"/literal
// else /keyword if a // //#/href ∈ /a //"ec"/literal // then /keyword //"escapeformat"/literal
// else /keyword // // let /keyword kk = // //#replaceWords:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word /href replaceWords /a([a], b)
// assert /keyword // //#not:boolean:boolean /href not /a.isempty.kk // report /keyword //"KK"/literal,
kk /block

function // build:int:convertInfo:state:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word /id // #PEGgenNoTable /href /a build(
level:int
, info:convertInfo
, state:state
, stk:seq.word
, i:seq.word
, inputi:seq.word
, result:seq.word
, faili:seq.word
, failini:seq.word
, failresult:seq.word
) seq.word
// let /keyword next = //"next(/trace stepno+1, trace, /end"/literal,
// if /keyword level // //#>:int:int:boolean /href > /a 0
// //#∧:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∧ /a (state // //#/href ∈ /a newStates.info // //#∨:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∨ /a state // //#=:state:state:boolean /href = /a start.info) // then /keyword // next
// //#/href+/a stk
// //#/href+/a //","/literal
// //#/href+/a // //#%:int:seq.word /href % /a.// //#cvt:convertInfo:state:int:int /href cvt /a(info, state, 4)
// //#/href+/a //","/literal
// //#/href+/a i
// //#/href+/a //","/literal
// //#/href+/a inputi
// //#/href+/a //","/literal
// //#/href+/a result
// //#/href+/a //","/literal
// //#/href+/a faili
// //#/href+/a //","/literal
// //#/href+/a failini
// //#/href+/a //","/literal
// //#/href+/a failresult

// // prettyScript /id Module /keyword prettyScript

Implement /em prettyScript command.

use file

use seq1.filename

use makescript

use standard

use svg

use arc.word

use drawGraph.arc.word

use graph.arc.word

use set.arc.word

use seq1.seq.word

use seq1.word

use set.word

Function // prettyScript:seq.file:seq.word:seq.file:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word /id // #prettyScript /href /a prettyScript(
input:seq.file
, builddir:seq.word
, hashes:seq.file
, roots:seq.word
, %:seq.word
) seq.word
//{COMMAND /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp prettyScript /tag </strong> pretty prints /sp.bld script./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp % /tag </strong> format of output. /sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
pretty /tag </strong> ? /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp full /tag </strong> Graph of dependences between files./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp outdated /tag </strong> like full but only include outdated files in graph /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp script /tag </strong> shell script for updating without the file hashes /sp /tag <span /sp class /nsp ="/nsp block"/tag > /nsp. /tag </span> /sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp
options used for formats other than pretty./sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp roots /tag </strong> only include the filenames list and their descendants in graph. /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp hashes /tag </strong> ? /sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp
/sp /tag <strong /tag > /nsp builddir /tag </strong> build target directory. Defaults to"+built"}/comment
// let /keyword format = (// if /keyword isempty.% // then /keyword //"pretty"/literal // else /keyword %) // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1,
// if /keyword format // //#/href ∈ /a //"pretty"/literal // then /keyword // //#pretty:seq.file:seq.word /href pretty /a.input
// else /keyword // // let /keyword txt = // // //#makeScriptCore:seq.file:seq.word:word:seq.file:seq.word /href makeScriptCore /a(input, // if /keyword isempty.builddir // then /keyword //"+built"/literal // else /keyword builddir, format, hashes), /block // if /keyword format // //#/href ∈ /a //"script"/literal // then /keyword txt
// else /keyword // // let /keyword asfn = // //#tofilenames:seq.word:seq.filename /href tofilenames /a.txt
// for /keyword // acc = empty:seq.arc.word
, tail = false
, last = asfn // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, fn ∈ asfn /block // do /keyword // next(// // if /keyword tail // then /keyword // acc
// //#/href+/a arc(// // //#fullname:filename:word /href fullname /a.last
, // //#fullname:filename:word /href fullname /a.fn /block)/block // else /keyword acc
, // //#not:boolean:boolean /href not /a.tail
, fn /block)/block // let /keyword g2 = // //#newgraph:seq.T:graph.T /href newgraph /a.acc,
// let /keyword g3 = // // if /keyword isempty.roots // then /keyword g2
// else /keyword // // //#subgraph:graph.T:set.<<.T:graph.T /href subgraph /a(// g2
, // //#/href reachable /a(// g2
, // //#fullnames:seq.filename:seq.word /href fullnames /a.// //#tofilenames:seq.word:seq.filename /href tofilenames /a.roots /block)/block), /block /block // //#drawgraph:seq.T:set.<<.T:set.<<.T:seq.word /href drawgraph /a(// toseq.arcs.g3
, // //#empty::set.T:set.T /href empty:set.word /a
, // //#empty::set.T:set.T /href empty:set.word /a /block)/block /block

______________

function // width:word:int /id // #prettyScript /href /a width(w:word) int
// if /keyword w // //#/href ∈ /a //".+"/literal // then /keyword 1 // else /keyword n.// //#decodeword:word:seq.char /href decodeword /a.w // //#+:int:int:int /href+/a 1

Function // pretty:seq.file:seq.word /id // #prettyScript /href /a pretty(input:seq.file) seq.word
// for /keyword acc = //""/literal, l ∈ // //#breakparagraph:seq.file:seq.seq.word /href breakparagraph /a.input
// do /keyword // // if /keyword // //#subseq:seq.T:int:int:seq.T /href subseq /a(l, 1, 2) = //"# File"/literal // then /keyword acc
// else /keyword if // //#subseq:seq.T:int:int:seq.T /href subseq /a(l, 1, 1) // //#/href ∈ /a [//"#"/literal, //"define"/literal] // then /keyword acc // //#/href+/a l // //#/href+/a //"/sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp"/literal
// else /keyword // // for /keyword // all = empty:seq.seq.word
, part = //""/literal
, width = 0
, last = l // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
, w ∈ l // //#<<:seq.T:int:seq.T /href << /a 1 /block // do /keyword // // if /keyword width // //#>:int:int:boolean /href > /a 90 // //#∧:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∧ /a last ∉ //"."/literal
// //#∨:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∨ /a last // //#/href ∈ /a //"+"/literal
// //#∧:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∧ /a // //#not:boolean:boolean /href not /a.isempty.part
// //#∧:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∧ /a part // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a n.part ∉ //":: "/literal
// //#∨:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∨ /a w // //#/href ∈ /a //":: "/literal // then /keyword next(all // //#/href+/a part, [last], // //#width:word:int /href width /a.last, w)
// else /keyword next(all, part // //#/href+/a last, width // //#+:int:int:int /href+/a // //#width:word:int /href width /a.last, w)/block // let /keyword all1 = all // //#/href+/a (part // //#/href+/a last)
// let /keyword all2 = // // if /keyword n.all1 // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1 // //#=:int:int:boolean /href = /a 1 // then /keyword // all1 // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
// //#/href+/a // //#/href % /a(//"/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp"/literal, all1 // //#<<:seq.T:int:seq.T /href << /a 1) // //#>>:seq.T:int:seq.T /href >> /a 1 /block // else /keyword // //#/href % /a(//"/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp"/literal, all1) // //#>>:seq.T:int:seq.T /href >> /a 1, /block // let /keyword txt = // // if /keyword // //#subseq:seq.T:int:int:seq.T /href subseq /a(all2, 2, 2) = //"+"/literal // then /keyword // // //#%:word:seq.word /href % /a.all2 // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1
// //#/href+/a //"/sp"/literal
// //#/href+/a all2 // //#<<:seq.T:int:seq.T /href << /a 1 /block // else /keyword all2, /block acc // //#/href+/a txt // //#/href+/a //"/sp /tag <p /tag > /nsp"/literal, /block /block acc

// // backparse3 /id Module /keyword backparse3

use standard

use seq1.symbol

use symbol1

Function // backparse3:seq.symbol:int:boolean:int /id // #backparse3 /href /a backparse3(s:seq.symbol, ii:int, includeDefine:boolean) int
// let /keyword i = ii
// assert /keyword i // //#>:int:int:boolean /href > /a 0 // report /keyword //"back parse 1a::(s):(stacktrace)"/literal
// let /keyword kind = // //#kind:symbol:symbolKind /href kind /a.s // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a i,
// if /keyword kind // //#=:symbolKind:symbolKind:boolean /href = /a // //#kendblock:symbolKind /href kendblock /a // then /keyword // //#matchblock:seq.symbol:int:int:int /href matchblock /a(s, i // //#/href-/a 1, 0)
// else /keyword // // let /keyword i1 = // // if /keyword kind // //#=:symbolKind:symbolKind:boolean /href = /a // //#kjmp:symbolKind /href kjmp /a // then /keyword i // //#/href-/a // //#value:symbol:int /href value /a.s // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a i
// else /keyword i /block // for /keyword // k = i1
, j ∈ // //#constantseq:int:T:seq.T /href constantseq /a(// //#nopara:symbol:int /href nopara /a.s // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a i1, 1)/block // do /keyword // //#backparse3:seq.symbol:int:boolean:int /href backparse3 /a(s, k // //#/href-/a 1, includeDefine),
// if /keyword includeDefine // then /keyword // //#addDefine:seq.symbol:int:int /href addDefine /a(s, k) // else /keyword k /block

function // matchblock:seq.symbol:int:int:int /id // #backparse3 /href /a matchblock(s:seq.symbol, i:int, nest:int) int
// let /keyword sym = s // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a i
// let /keyword kind = // //#kind:symbol:symbolKind /href kind /a.sym
// let /keyword newnest = // // if /keyword kind // //#/href ∈ /a [// //#kloop:symbolKind /href kloop /a, // //#kstart:symbolKind /href kstart /a] // then /keyword nest // //#/href-/a 1
// else /keyword if kind // //#=:symbolKind:symbolKind:boolean /href = /a // //#kendblock:symbolKind /href kendblock /a // then /keyword nest // //#+:int:int:int /href+/a 1
// else /keyword nest, /block // if /keyword nest // //#=:int:int:boolean /href = /a 0 // then /keyword // // if /keyword kind // //#=:symbolKind:symbolKind:boolean /href = /a // //#kloop:symbolKind /href kloop /a // then /keyword // // for /keyword // k = i
, j ∈ // //#constantseq:int:T:seq.T /href constantseq /a(// //#nopara:symbol:int /href nopara /a.sym, 1)/block // do /keyword // //#backparse3:seq.symbol:int:boolean:int /href backparse3 /a(s, k // //#/href-/a 1, false),
k /block // else /keyword if kind // //#=:symbolKind:symbolKind:boolean /href = /a // //#kstart:symbolKind /href kstart /a // then /keyword // //#addDefine:seq.symbol:int:int /href addDefine /a(s, i)
// else /keyword // //#matchblock:seq.symbol:int:int:int /href matchblock /a(s, i // //#/href-/a 1, newnest)/block // else /keyword // //#matchblock:seq.symbol:int:int:int /href matchblock /a(s, i // //#/href-/a 1, newnest)

function // addDefine:seq.symbol:int:int /id // #backparse3 /href /a addDefine(s:seq.symbol, i:int) int
// if /keyword i // //#>:int:int:boolean /href > /a 1
// //#∧:boolean:boolean:boolean /href ∧ /a // //#kind:symbol:symbolKind /href kind /a.s // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a (i // //#/href-/a 1)
// //#=:symbolKind:symbolKind:boolean /href = /a // //#kdefine:symbolKind /href kdefine /a // then /keyword // //#addDefine:seq.symbol:int:int /href addDefine /a(s, // //#backparse3:seq.symbol:int:boolean:int /href backparse3 /a(s, i // //#/href-/a 2, false))
// else /keyword i

// // svg /id Module /keyword svg

use seq1.seq.int

use sort.seq.int

use seq1.int

use layernode

use seq.layernode

use standard

use uniqueids

Function // text:seq.word:seq.word:int:int:seq.word:seq.word /id // #svg /href /a text(class:seq.word, id:seq.word, x:int, y:int, w:seq.word) seq.word
//{"/tag <text /sp class = /tag:(dq.class):(if id =""then id else"id = /tag:(dq.id)")x = /tag:(dq.%.x)y = /tag:(dq.%.y)>:(w)/tag </text>"}/comment
// //#atts:seq.word:seq.word /href atts /a.//"/tag <text = class =:(class)=:(// if /keyword id = //""/literal // then /keyword id // else /keyword //"id =:(id)="/literal)x =:(x)= y =:(y)/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp"/literal
// //#/href+/a //">:(w)/tag </text>"/literal

Function // svgpath:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word:seq.word /id // #svg /href /a svgpath(class:seq.word, id:seq.word, path:seq.word) seq.word
// //#atts:seq.word:seq.word /href atts /a.//"/tag <path = class =:(class)=:(// if /keyword id = //""/literal // then /keyword id // else /keyword //"id =:(id)="/literal)d =:(path)/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp />"/literal

""

Function // svg:seq.seq.word:seq.seq.word:seq.word:seq.word /id // #svg /href /a svg(classes2:seq.seq.word, otherAttributes:seq.seq.word, body:seq.word) seq.word
// for /keyword att = //""/literal, e ∈ otherAttributes
// do /keyword // // if /keyword isempty.e // then /keyword //""/literal
// else /keyword // att
// //#/href+/a //":(e // //#sub:seq.T:int:T /href sub /a 1)=:(// //#/href dq /a.//"/nsp:(e // //#<<:seq.T:int:seq.T /href << /a 1)"/literal)"/literal /block /block // for /keyword // acc = //""/literal
, e ∈ // for /keyword acc2 = empty:seq.seq.word, e ∈ classes2 // do /keyword acc2 // //#/href+/a //""/literal // //#/href+/a e,
acc2 /block // do /keyword acc // //#/href+/a e,
//"/tag <svg /sp:(att)> /tag <style>:(acc)/tag ????</style>:(body)Your browser does not support inline SVG. /tag </svg>"/literal

Function // drawscript:seq.word /id // #svg /href /a drawscript seq.word
//{Adjust beginning of paths so the start at the end of the word}/comment
//"/tag <script> function shiftstart(arcs){let bb = document.getElementById(arcs[0]).getBBox(); arcs.forEach(function(idval, index){if(index > 0){let element = document.getElementById(idval); let d = /tag:(// //#/href dq /a.//"M"/literal)+(bb.x+bb.width)+/tag:(// //#/href dq /a.//","/literal)+(bb.y+bb.height / 2)+element.getAttribute(/tag:(// //#/href dq /a.//"/nsp d"/literal)).substring(5); element.setAttribute(/tag:(// //#/href dq /a.//"/nsp d"/literal), d);}});}/tag </script>"/literal
// //#/href+/a // //#encodeword:seq.char:word /href encodeword /a.[char.10]

Function // atts:seq.word:seq.word /id // #svg /href /a atts(s:seq.word) seq.word
// for /keyword state = 0, acc = //""/literal, acc2 = //""/literal, w ∈ s

/tag ></rect><text = pointer-events = none = class = nodes = x =:(nodex)= y =:(nodey)= opacity = 0.0 = x =:(nodex)= y =:(nodey)/sp /tag <br /tag > /nsp >:(hovertext)/tag </text></g>"/literal

function // %:arc.<<.T:seq.word /id // #drawGraph /href /a %(a:arc.<<.T) seq.word //"{:(tail.a):(head.a)}"/literal
